title: The History of Stamps
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text:
Before postage stamps were invented, sending mail was an expensive and complicated (0). . The recipient usually had to pay for delivery, which meant that many letters were simply refused. In 1840, Britain (1). the world's first adhesive postage stamp, the Penny Black, revolutionising postal services forever.

The new system was an immediate success and quickly (2). on across Europe and beyond. Stamps made it possible for ordinary people to (3). in touch with distant relatives and friends. The simple act of prepaying postage transformed communication and helped (4). literacy rates as people had more reasons to write.

Today, stamp collecting remains one of the world's most popular hobbies. Rare stamps can (5). for enormous sums at auction, with some fetching millions of pounds. Collectors are drawn to the historical significance and artistic (6). of these tiny pieces of paper. Despite the (7). of email and digital communication, stamps continue to be produced and valued. Many countries issue special commemorative stamps to (8). important events and famous figures.

example.explanation: 'Affair' se usa para describir un asunto o situación compleja.

questions.explanation: 'Introduce' es el verbo correcto para presentar algo nuevo al público.
'Catch on' significa volverse popular o extenderse.
'Keep in touch' es la expresión fija para mantener contacto.
'Raise rates' es la colocación correcta para aumentar tasas o índices.
'Sell for' indica el precio por el que algo se vende.
'Artistic value' es la colocación correcta para valor artístico.
'Rise of' es la expresión correcta para el surgimiento de algo.
'Mark an event' significa conmemorar o señalar un evento importante.
